title: Normalize any non-zero value to exactly 1 using iszero(iszero(x))
description: iszero(x) returns 1 if x=0, else 0. iszero(iszero(x)) returns 0 if x=0, else 1. This cleans any non-zero uint256 to exactly 1 in two opcodes, critical when storing booleans in packed slots where a value of 2 would corrupt adjacent fields.
pattern_before:
// Risky — stores 2 if isApproved=2, corrupts packed slot
sstore(slot, isApproved)
when_to_apply: Any bool value coming from external input before storing in a packed slot
when_not_to_apply: Values already known to be 0 or 1 from prior operations
